channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bihar Student Speaks on Unfinished AIIMS and NEET-UG Exam Paper Leak
item.description: The article reports the concerns of a student from Bihar regarding the recent leak of unfinished AIIMS and NEET-UG examination papers. The boy expressed frustration over the lack of attention from aut...

item.title: Everyday Life in the Dutch East Indies: Vintage Photos from Early 20th-Century Indonesia
item.description: This collection of vintage photographs offers a vivid window into everyday life in Indonesia during the early 20th century, when the archipelago was known as the Dutch East Indies. The images, capture...

item.title: IHC Seeks Proper Filing of SC Orders in Social Media Case Involving Convicted Lawyers

item.title: The New York Times Challenges Pentagon Press Restrictions Under Hegseth
item.description: The New York Times has filed a lawsuit against Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth regarding his Pentagon press access policy, which critics argue undermines press freedom. Under this policy, journalist...

item.title: Bluefins swimmers perform strongly at FK Blue Marlines competition
item.description: In a recent swimming event organized by FK Blue Marlines, the young athletes from Bluefins club delivered an outstanding performance. Swimmers showcased their skill and dedication, earning recognition...

item.title: Malacañang urges Vice President Sara Duterte to respond to impeachment allegations
item.description: Malacañang has called on Vice President Sara Duterte to confront the allegations filed against her after the Senate convened as an impeachment court. Palace Press Officer Undersecretary Claire Castro ...

item.title: The Strategic Challenges Facing the U.S. in Iran
item.description: The article analyzes the growing difficulties the United States faces in Iran under President Trump’s administration. It argues that U.S. strategies have repeatedly failed, leaving no option for a suc...

item.title: Debate in the UK over dogs in public spaces and boundaries for pet owners
item.description: The UK is experiencing a noticeable rise in the number of dogs in public spaces, prompting debate over hygiene, safety, and social etiquette. Influencer Gizzelle Cade recounted an incident in a London...

item.title: Cindy Williams Remembered: Iconic Actress Known for Laverne & Shirley and American Graffiti
item.description: Cindy Williams, born in 1947 and passed away in 2023, was an American actress celebrated for her memorable performances that captured the heart of classic television and film comedy. She rose to wides...

item.title: Digital Transformation Drives Growth in South Africa’s Betting Market
item.description: South Africa’s betting industry is experiencing a major shift due to digitalisation, with mobile accessibility playing a key role in its rapid growth. Online betting now accounts for the majority of w...

item.title: Senior Bureau of Meteorology Executive to Retire Following Website Overhaul Controversy

item.title: Max Scherzer's Unique Eye Colours Explained
item.description: Max Scherzer, the American baseball star, is famous not just for his pitching but also for his distinctive eyes. He has complete heterochromia iridis, which means his left eye is brown and right eye i...

item.title: Rising Afrophobia in South Africa and the Need for Legal and Political Action
item.description: Dis my brother/sis, the situation in South Africa dey tense, as hatred against other Africans dey rise. Sarah Dajen explain say what dey happen no be ordinary xenophobia, na afrophobia, because e dey ...

item.title: Supreme Court Upholds Order on Removal of Stray Dogs from Public Institutions, States Urged to Comply
item.description: The Supreme Court of India on Tuesday refused to stay its earlier order that mandates the removal of stray dogs from public spaces such as hospitals, schools, and colleges. This decision came after pl...

item.title: Naira Records Slight Depreciation Against Dollar at NFEM Window on May 19, 2026

item.title: Ontario Teen Reported Missing Found Dead in Northern Saskatchewan; Police Treat Case as Homicide
item.description: Authorities in northern Saskatchewan have confirmed the body of a missing teenager from Ontario has been found, and the death is being treated as a homicide. The teen, whose disappearance prompted a w...

item.title: Manchester Romcom ‘Finding Emily’ Celebrates City’s Musical Heritage
item.description: ‘Finding Emily’ is a charming new romcom set in Manchester, crafted by producers of Love Actually. Starring Angourie Rice and Spike Fearn, the film follows Owen, a young musician, who embarks on a cam...

item.title: Relief Efforts Underway for Cape Farmers After Severe Floods
item.description: Recent heavy storms have caused severe flooding across the Western and Eastern Cape, resulting in the tragic deaths of 11 people and widespread damage to farms and communities. Crops have been destroy...

item.title: George Harrison Marks His 21st Birthday with Fans in London, 1964
item.description: On February 25, 1964, George Harrison celebrated his 21st birthday in a meaningful and personal way, despite The Beatles' rising global fame. Instead of a lavish party, he chose to visit The Beatles’ ...
